reasoning: Query asks for cherry blossom spots in Tohoku; tool fetches generic Akita spots with no sakura semantic tagging. Kubota Castle is a famous sakura spot but appears without cherry blossom context. No other Tohoku prefectures fetched (Iwate/Miyagi/Aomori/Yamagata/Fukushima omitted). Response is well-formed Wikidata data but misses the seasonal/thematic constraint entirely.
missing_data: Hirosaki Castle (Aomori), Kakunodate cherry blossoms (Akita), Kitakami Tenshochi (Iwate), and other Tohoku prefectures. No sakura-specific keyword or seasonal metadata.
improvement_hint: Add cherry blossom semantic tag to spots like Kubota Castle and Kakunodate; expand retrieval to all Tohoku prefectures or use search_area with '桜' keyword to surface sakura-relevant results.

reasoning: Query asks for major sake breweries in Niigata — a world-famous sake prefecture. Tool fetches local_specialty with no category filter, returning MAFF GI food items (edamame, carrots, lotus root). No sake brewery data exists in this tool's scope. Complete retrieval mismatch — the MCP has no brewery POI database and get_local_specialty covers designated foods/crafts, not brewery directories.
missing_data: Koshi no Kanbai, Kubota, Hakkaisan and other famous Niigata kura. No sake brewery POI data available in current tool set.
improvement_hint: Add a sake/craft-beverage category to get_local_specialty covering GI-sake designations (Niigata sake has GI recognition); or add brewery POI scraping from municipal tourism pages that list sake-related tours.

reasoning: Query asks for ski resorts with hot springs near Hakuba. Response returns municipal navigation pages (観光課, 観光商工係) and a botanical garden — no ski resort or onsen data at all. The municipal scrape returns low-quality admin pages. Wikidata entries are a lake and art museum. Neither ski nor onsen constraints are addressed.
missing_data: Hakuba 47, Goryu, Cortina and other ski resorts; Hakuba Happo-one onsen; Tsugaike Kogen; no lodging_type=onsen_ryokan results surfaced.
improvement_hint: Supplement municipal scrape with get_hotels filtered to onsen_ryokan for Hakuba municipality; add ski area POI type to attract keyword from search_area with 'スキー場 白馬' query.

reasoning: Query asks for best autumn foliage spots in Kansai region. Tool fetches only Kyoto (covering 1 of ~5 Kansai prefectures). Kyoto is the premier foliage destination so coverage is partially useful, but Osaka, Nara, Hyogo, Shiga are omitted. Data quality is high — Nijo Castle, Toji, Nishihonganji, Ninnaji, Saihoji all returned with full heritage labels and prominence scores. No autumn semantic tagging.
missing_data: Osaka (Mino-o Park), Nara (Yoshino, Kasugayama), Hyogo (Kiyomizu Temple Himeji), Shiga (Enryakuji). Only Kyoto queried for a multi-prefecture region.
improvement_hint: For regional queries, issue parallel get_spots for all constituent prefectures; add seasonal/foliage keyword tagging to relevant spots (Saihoji and Ninnaji are famous koyo sites).

reasoning: Query (Arabic) asks for famous cherry blossom spots in Kansai. Tool fetches Nara spots — relevant since Nara has famous sakura (Yoshino especially), but only Nara is covered. Returned spots are shrines/temples tagged as 神社・仏閣 without cherry blossom attribution. Yoshino mountain (the most famous sakura site in Kansai) is absent. Kyoto, Osaka omitted.
missing_data: Yoshino Mikumari Shrine and Yoshino mountain sakura (the most famous Kansai cherry blossom site), Kyoto Maruyama Park, Osaka Expo Kinen Park. No sakura-specific tagging.
improvement_hint: Add 桜 keyword search capability layered on spot retrieval; ensure Yoshino is tagged with cherry blossom metadata; expand to multi-prefecture for Kansai region queries.

reasoning: Query asks for remote island stargazing spots in Okinawa. Response returns main island cultural heritage (Shuri Castle, Tamaudun, Nakagusuku Castle, Sefa-utaki, Nakijin Castle) — none are remote islands or dark-sky sites. Iriomote, Ishigaki, Miyako (top stargazing islands) are absent. Complete constraint mismatch on both 'remote island' and 'stargazing' dimensions.
missing_data: Iriomote Island, Ishigaki Island (IAU-designated dark sky area), Miyako Island, Yoron Island — all famous for low light pollution. No island-type POI differentiation exists.
improvement_hint: Add island municipality filter capability; tag dark-sky or astronomy-relevant spots; include Yaeyama/Miyako island municipalities in retrieval scope for stargazing queries.

reasoning: Query (Thai) asks for rural onsen accommodation in Hokkaido. Tool returns urban city hotels dominated by Sapporo and Kushiro properties — ANA chains, urban business hotels. No onsen_ryokan lodging_type filtering applied. Rural constraint entirely ignored. Niseko, Noboribetsu, Jozankei, Sounkyo onsen areas absent.
missing_data: Noboribetsu Daiichi Takimotokan, Jozankei onsen ryokan, Sounkyo onsen, Toyako onsen — major rural onsen destinations. No onsen_ryokan filter applied.
improvement_hint: Add lodging_type filter parameter to get_hotels; prioritize onsen_ryokan type results; add rural/remote classification to properties outside major cities.

reasoning: Query (Vietnamese) asks for islands in the Seto Inland Sea. Tool fetches Kagawa spots — returning Ritsurin Garden, Marugame Castle, Takamatsu Castle, Kotohira-gu. These are on the main Shikoku island, not Seto Inland Sea islands. Naoshima, Teshima, Shodoshima (all Kagawa's famous islands) are absent. The island constraint is not met.
missing_data: Naoshima (art island), Shodoshima, Teshima, Ogijima, Megijima — all Kagawa's Seto Inland Sea islands famous for art and scenery. No island-type differentiation.
improvement_hint: Add island-municipality routing to get_spots; tag POIs on islands distinctly from mainland; search_area with '瀬戸内海 島' would better serve this intent.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima. Tool correctly uses get_local_specialty with craft filter. Returns 3 items: Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave (indigo textile — direct hit), Otani Ware (ceramics, tangential), Awa Washi (paper mentioning indigo). Official METI designations with good descriptions. Production area text helps localize. Not workshop listings, but the best available official data.
missing_data: Workshop/experience booking information; Awa Ai indigo dye itself (the plant) is not listed; no specific workshop venues or addresses for visitor experiences.
improvement_hint: Add experience/workshop links from municipal tourism pages; include Awa Ai (藍) plant GI or related specialty; note that 'workshop' requires cross-referencing with spot data from municipal scrapes.

reasoning: Query (German) asks for Buddhist temple lodging (shukubo) on Mount Koya. Tool returns generic Wakayama hotels — urban hotels in Tanabe, guest houses with no address, Nanki-Shirahama Marriott. Zero shukubo or Koyasan-area lodging. The hotels database lacks a temple/shukubo lodging type. Mount Koya has 50+ shukubo but none appear.
missing_data: Ekoin, Eko-in, Shojoshin-in and other Koyasan shukubo. No temple-lodging type in the hotels corpus.
improvement_hint: Add shukubo/temple_lodging as a lodging_type; scrape Koyasan municipal tourism or the official shukubo association site; add municipality filter for 高野町 to isolate Koyasan results.

reasoning: Query asks for samurai districts outside Kyoto. search_area with '武家屋敷' returns 82 matches with excellent geographic diversity: Chiran (Kagoshima), Aizu (Fukushima), Kanazawa (Ishikawa), Matsue (Shimane). Heritage designations (Important Preservation District) present. The off-Kyoto constraint is naturally satisfied by geographic spread. kinds metadata and prominence scores useful for agent ranking.
missing_data: Kakunodate (Akita) — a famous samurai district — not clearly visible in truncated results. Coordinates null for some entries.
improvement_hint: Ensure Kakunodate appears in results; add 'not in prefecture X' filter capability; include opening hours or access info from municipal scrapes for popular sites like Chiran.

reasoning: Query (Indonesian) asks for summer festivals in Akita. Tool returns only 3 festivals (Dainichido Bugaku, Kakunodate Festival, Tsuchizaki Shrine Festival) — all are Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties with no dates. Critically missing Akita Kanto Festival (Nebuta equivalent, UNESCO-listed, held August 3-6) which is the most famous Akita summer festival. Coverage very sparse (3 items).
missing_data: Akita Kanto Matsuri (竿燈まつり) — one of Tohoku's Three Great Festivals, held early August. Also Noshiro Neburi Nagashi. No specific summer dates for any of the 3 returned festivals.
improvement_hint: Supplement bunka_intangible source with municipal event scraping to capture Kanto Festival; add date filtering for summer months; the festival count of 3 for a prefecture famous for matsuri indicates coverage gap.

reasoning: Query (Korean) asks for small fishing port towns in the Sanin region. Tool returns Shimane general spots dominated by Iwami Ginzan (mine), Matsue Castle, Gassantoda Castle, Izumo Taisha — cultural/historic heritage, not fishing ports. Masuda, Hama-da, Gotsu as fishing port towns are absent. The 'fishing port' and 'small town' constraints are entirely unmet.
missing_data: Oda, Hamada, Masuda coastal fishing areas; Tottori coast fishing ports. No coastal/port POI type differentiation in the data.
improvement_hint: Add port/fishing-community POI type; supplement Wikidata with municipal scrapes from coastal towns; search_area with '漁港' keyword would better serve this intent than prefecture-wide get_spots.

reasoning: Query asks where to experience washi paper making in Gifu. Tool returns 8 crafts including Mino Ware, Hida Shunkei lacquerware, Nagiso Lathe Work, Ichii Ittobori — these are good Gifu traditional crafts but washi (Mino Washi) should be the top result. The truncated response at 4000 chars suggests Mino Washi appears later in the list. Production area text gives localization. No workshop experience links.
missing_data: Mino Washi (美濃和紙) — expected primary result — may appear later in list but not in truncated view. No workshop/experience venue links.
improvement_hint: Rank washi-category results higher when query contains 'washi'; include workshop experience URLs from municipal scrapes; Mino Washi is METI-designated so should appear in craft results.

reasoning: Query (Spanish) asks for fishing villages on the Noto Peninsula. Tool returns Ishikawa spots heavily biased toward Kanazawa city (Kanazawa Castle, Kenroku-en, National Crafts Museum, Oyama Shrine). Noto Peninsula fishing communities (Wajima, Suzu, Monzen) are absent. The peninsula-specific constraint and 'fishing village' theme are not met.
missing_data: Wajima (朝市 morning market), Suzu salt farms, Monzen fishing community, Noto Kiriko lantern festival areas — all Noto Peninsula fishing/coastal culture sites.
improvement_hint: Add municipality sub-filter for Noto Peninsula cities (Wajima, Suzu, Nanao); search_area with '能登 漁村' would surface more targeted results; add coastal/fishing community tags.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes beyond Tottori. search_area returns 80 results but dominated by Tottori dune content (first 3 results). Also returns 貝塚 (middens/shell mounds) named 高砂貝塚 which are archaeological sites, not sand dunes — semantic noise. Lesser-known alternatives (Sakata Dunes in Kanagawa, Nakatajima in Shizuoka, Sarufutsu in Hokkaido) are not identified.
missing_data: Nakatajima Sand Dunes (Shizuoka), Sakata Dunes (Kanagawa), Sarufutsu Sand Dunes (Hokkaido) — Japan's other famous dune sites. The 80 results likely contain them but ranking surfaces Tottori first.
wrong_data: 高砂貝塚 is a shell midden (archaeological site), not a sand dune — false semantic match.
improvement_hint: Improve semantic disambiguation between 砂丘 (dune) and 砂原/貝塚 (sandy plain/midden); add explicit sand dune POI type; filter Tottori results when user says 'beyond Tottori'.

reasoning: Query (Chinese) asks for traditional crafts in Sanin region. Tool returns 4 Shimane METI-designated crafts with full Chinese translations: Iwami Ware, Sekishu Washi (paper), Unshu Abacus, Izumo Stone Lanterns. All are accurate, complete with production area text and official descriptions in Chinese. The Tottori side of Sanin is omitted but Shimane coverage is excellent.
missing_data: Tottori crafts (Inshuu Washi paper, Tottori mingei pottery) — Sanin includes both Shimane and Tottori. Coverage is Shimane-only.
improvement_hint: For Sanin region queries, issue parallel calls for both Shimane and Tottori; add region-to-prefecture mapping for 'Sanin' (山陰) as a known region alias.

reasoning: Query asks for lavender fields in Hokkaido beyond Furano. Tool returns generic Hokkaido spots (Matsumae Castle, Goryokaku, Showa-Shinzan, Sapporo Clock Tower, Asahi Bridge, Daisetsuzan). Zero flower field or lavender data. The specific floral/seasonal constraint is entirely unaddressed. Biei and Kamifurano lavender areas absent.
missing_data: Biei Blue Pond area lavender, Kamifurano lavender fields, Nakafurano Hokusei-no-oka, Tokachi Millennium Forest — all lesser-known alternatives. No floral/seasonal POI type in corpus.
improvement_hint: Add seasonal flower event scraping from municipal tourism pages; tag spots with flower types; search_area with 'ラベンダー' would be more appropriate tool for this query.

reasoning: Query (Tagalog) asks for traditional townscapes/old towns in Kyushu (topic: 九州 古い町並み). Tool fetches Oita spots. While Oita has Usuki Stone Buddhas and some historic sites, major Kyushu historic townscapes (Yanagawa, Hita Mameda-machi, Chiran, Nagasaki Dejima) are absent. Only Oita covered. The result set lacks traditional townscape data.
missing_data: Hita Mameda-machi (Oita — ironically in the right prefecture), Yanagawa, Nagasaki historic district, Chiran (covered in L2-16 via search_area). Traditional townscape POI type missing.
improvement_hint: For townscape queries, use search_area with '伝建地区' (preservation district) or '古い町並み'; multi-prefecture coverage of Kyushu needed; Important Preservation Districts dataset should be primary source.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional old private houses (kominka) to stay in Hokuriku. Tool returns Ishikawa hotels: ANA Crowne Plaza, student housing, guest houses — no kominka. The lodging database has no kominka type. Fukui and Toyama (also Hokuriku) not queried. The specific accommodation type is entirely missing from the data model.
missing_data: Kominka stays in Noto Peninsula, Kaga area, Shirakawa-go (technically Gifu but near Hokuriku). No traditional farmhouse lodging type in the database.
wrong_data: Student housing (name_en: 'Student housing 1') is not relevant travel accommodation.
improvement_hint: Add kominka/traditional farmhouse as a lodging_type; scrape municipal tourism for minpaku (民泊) and traditional inn listings; extend to Toyama and Fukui for full Hokuriku coverage.

reasoning: Query asks for mountain shrines on the Kii Peninsula. Tool returns 7 Japan Heritage stories for Wakayama. First result (Wakanoura) is coastal scenery/poetry — not mountain shrines. The Saigoku Pilgrimage and Kumano Kodo heritage stories are likely among the 7 and directly relevant to mountain worship. Good institutional grounding via Agency for Cultural Affairs, but the mountain shrine constraint requires agent-side filtering.
missing_data: Kumano Hongu Taisha, Nachi Taisha, Hayatama Taisha (the three Kumano Grand Shrines) not explicitly named in truncated view. Koyasan is in Wakayama but requires separate tool call.
improvement_hint: Add relevance_score based on query keywords; Kumano Kodo heritage story should rank first for mountain shrine queries; include shrine/temple type tags within Japan Heritage stories.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks festivals along Sea of Japan coast. Tool returns 129 Niigata events including Nagaoka Fireworks Festival (one of Japan's top 3, held Aug 2-3 on Shinano River). Direct hit. Also returns non-fireworks spring events (芝桜, lily festivals). Dates present for events. Missing other Sea of Japan coast prefectures (Toyama, Ishikawa, Fukui, Akita, Yamagata).
missing_data: Other Sea of Japan coast prefecture fireworks: Toyama Fireworks, Fukui Festival. Niigata alone is not the full Sea of Japan coast.
improvement_hint: Add fireworks event type tag to enable filtering within 129-item list; extend to adjacent Sea of Japan coast prefectures (Yamagata, Akita, Toyama); 'location' field is null for most events — should include venue coordinates.

reasoning: Query asks for bamboo crafts in Oita. Tool returns 2 items: Hakata Ori (weaving — includes Oita in prefecture_codes) and Beppu Bamboo Crafts (直接 hit — Madake bamboo, 8 weaving techniques, production in Beppu/Oita). Beppu Takesaiku is exactly what was asked. Official METI data with high-confidence English translations. Hakata Ori is a weaving craft not bamboo but it is legitimately designated for Oita area.
missing_data: No experience/workshop venue links; only 2 craft designations — the corpus for Oita crafts is naturally small but complete for METI designations.
improvement_hint: Add bamboo craft workshop venues from Beppu municipal tourism pages; note that Hakata Ori is weaving, not bamboo, so agent should filter by industry_category '木工品・竹工品' for strict bamboo queries.

reasoning: Query asks for cycling routes through rural Shikoku. Tool returns Ehime general spots (Uwajima Castle, Matsuyama Castle, Ozu Castle, Uchiko-za theater, Bansuiso). These are historic sites, not cycling infrastructure or route data. Shimanami Kaido (one of Asia's most famous cycling routes, crossing Ehime via island bridges) is entirely absent. Route/activity constraint completely unmet.
missing_data: Shimanami Kaido cycling route, Ochi-gun coastal cycling, inland rural cycling in Kochi or Tokushima. No cycling POI or route type in the corpus.
improvement_hint: Add cycling route POI type; scrape Shimanami Kaido official tourism page; search_area with 'サイクリング しまなみ' would be better-suited; add all 4 Shikoku prefectures for region queries.

reasoning: Query asks where to see kabuki outside Tokyo and Kyoto. Tool returns all 183 traditional arts including Kabuki as a UNESCO item. Kabuki is present but as a national-level concept with no venue/location data. No way to filter by region or find specific theaters. The 'outside Tokyo/Kyoto' constraint cannot be evaluated. Konpira Grand Theater (Kagawa) and Uchiko-za (Ehime) are actual rural kabuki venues but not surfaced.
missing_data: Konpira Grand Theater (Kotohira, Kagawa — oldest kabuki theater), Uchiko-za (Ehime), Nagahama Kabuki — specific venues where kabuki is performed outside major cities.
improvement_hint: Add venue/performance location data to traditional arts entries; link kabuki to specific theater POIs (Konpira Grand Theater is a national landmark); add prefecture_code filter to get_traditional_arts for regional queries.
